recipe.title: Elote Chicken Pasta Salad

ingredients.quantity: 2, seeds and stems removed, finely diced

steps: Pat chicken breast dry with a paper towel. In a small bowl mix together salt, paprika, chili powder, garlic powder, and onion powder. Drizzle chicken with olive oil and rub the spice mixture all over until every inch is evenly coated.

Heat a drizzle of olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat until shimmering. Add the chicken and cook undisturbed for 6–7 minutes until a deep golden crust forms on the bottom. Flip and cook another 6–7 minutes until cooked through and internal temp reaches 165°F. Transfer to a cutting board and let rest for 5 minutes before dicing into bite-sized pieces — resting keeps it juicy.
In a large bowl, whisk together sour cream, chipotle mayo, and lime juice until smooth and combined.
Add the cooked rotini, roasted corn, red onion, and jalapeños to the bowl with the dressing. Toss well until everything is evenly coated. Add the diced chicken and toss again gently to combine.
Fold in the cotija and cilantro last so they don't get crushed. Taste and season generously with salt, black pepper, and chili powder.
Serve immediately at room temperature or cover and chill in the fridge for at least 30 minutes for the flavors to really come together.

recipe.title: Grilled Hot Honey Chicken with Fresh Peach Salsa
steps: Preheat grill to medium-high heat.
Pat chicken breasts dry and season with salt and pepper.
Brush chicken with olive oil and place on grill.
Grill for 6-7 minutes per side until cooked through.
During the last minute of cooking, brush chicken generously with hot honey on both sides.
Remove chicken from grill and let rest for 5 minutes.
Meanwhile, prepare peach salsa by combining diced peaches, red onion, jalapeño, cilantro, and lime juice in a bowl.
Season salsa with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve grilled hot honey chicken topped with fresh peach salsa.

recipe.title: Avgolemono: Greek Lemon Chicken Soup

ingredients.item: Extra Virgin Olive Oil (I used Private Reserve Greek olive oil)
to 1 cup finely chopped green onions
cooked boneless chicken breast pieces, shredded ( about 6 to 8 oz store-bought rotisserie chicken will work)

steps: In a large Dutch oven or heavy pot, heat 1 tbsp olive oil on medium-high. Add the carrots, celery and green onions, toss together to saute briefly then stir in the garlic.

Add the chicken broth and bay leaves then raise the heat to high. Once the liquid has come to a rolling boil, add the rice, salt and pepper. Turn the heat to medium-low and simmer for 20 minutes or until the rice is tender. Now stir in the cooked chicken.

To prepare the egg-lemon sauce, in a medium bowl, whisk together the lemon juice and eggs. While whisking add 2 ladles-full of the broth from the cooking pot (this helps temper the eggs). Once fully combined, add the sauce to the chicken soup and stir. Remove from the heat immediately.
Garnish with fresh parsley, if you like. Serve hot with your favorite bread. Enjoy!

recipe.title: Makhani Masala Pasta Recipe

ingredients.item: chopped mixed vegetables - I used frozen mix veggie mix
½ teaspoon red chili powder - adjust per taste
cashews (make a paste by blending in 1/2 cup water)
extra cardamom powder and garam masala to sprinkle over the top.

steps: Press the sauté button on the Instant Pot. Once it displays hot, add the oil and then add the chopped garlic, ginger and onion. Sprinkle some salt. Sauté for 2 to 3 minutes until onions are soft.
Add a can of tomato puree, veggies, cardamom powder, garam masala, red chili powder. Mix well and let it cook for few minutes till the sauce starts to thicken.
Add the water and de-glaze the pot. Make sure there's nothing stuck at the bottom.
Add the pasta. Do NOT stir. Close the pot with its lid. Valve at SEALING. Press the MANUAL or PRESSURE COOK button and cook on high pressure for 5 minutes.
After it's done cooking, let the pressure release naturally for 5 minutes and then quick release the pressure. Once the silver pin drops, open the lid, stir everything and press the saute button.
Add the cashew paste. Crush kasuri methi on your palm and add it in the sauce. Let the pasta simmer for 2 minutes. Sprinkle chopped cilantro.
For additional flavor, sprinkle freshly ground black pepper, cardamom powder and garam masala on top before serving. Serve warm.
STOVE TOP INSTRUCTIONS
Prepare pasta according to the package instructions. Once boiled and ready, drain the water in colander and set it aside.
Heat a pan on medium heat and once hot, add oil to it. Then add garlic, ginger and onion. Sprinkle some salt. Cook for 2 to 3 minutes until softened.
Then add tomato sauce, veggies, cardamom powder, garam masala and red chili powder. Stir well and cook for 2 to 3 minutes until the raw smell goes away.
Add the cashew paste and mix. Add in the boiled pasta and mix well. You may have to add extra warm water if the sauce looks very thick. Adjust the seasoning. Let the pasta heat through.
Crush some kasuri methi on your palm and sprinkle it over. Turn off heat. Sprinkle freshly ground black pepper, cardamom powder, garam masala over the pasta just before serving.
Garnish with fresh chopped cilantro and serve warm.

recipe.title: Masala Pasta (Indian Style Pasta)

ingredients.item: curry powder (see substitutes in the post)

steps: Boil pasta according to packet instructions. Drain and reserve some of the pasta water.
Heat oil in a pan and add the garlic. Sauté for a few seconds then tip in the onions. Cook until the onions have softened.
Add the tomato puree and stir. Add the spices - chilli flakes, kashmiri chilli powder and curry powder. Season with salt. Pour over some pasta water to prevent the spices burning.
Pour over the cream, mix to combine and stir through the pasta. Toss to combine and add more pasta water to loosen.
Add the cheese and mix. Garnish with coriander if using and serve.

recipe.title: Masala Macaroni with Peas and Carrots
steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to a rolling boil over high heat.
Add the elbow macaroni and cook for 8 minutes exactly, stirring occasionally to prevent sticking.
Drain the pasta thoroughly, reserving ½ cup of the starchy cooking water.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat until it shimmers, about 1 minute.
Add the chopped onion and cook, stirring frequently, until translucent and lightly golden, 5-6 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and grated ginger, cooking until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Sprinkle in the garam masala, turmeric, and cayenne, toasting the spices for 45 seconds until deeply aromatic.
Add the diced carrots and frozen peas, stirring to coat them in the spiced oil.
Pour in the diced tomatoes with their juices and the heavy cream, stirring to combine.
Bring the sauce to a gentle simmer, then reduce heat to low and cook for 8 minutes until the carrots are tender-crisp.
Stir in the drained macaroni and salt, adding reserved pasta water 1 tablespoon at a time until the sauce reaches your desired consistency.
Remove from heat and fold in the chopped cilantro.

recipe.title: Masala Macaroni
steps: Bring 4 cups of water to a boil in a large pot over high heat, then add 1 tsp salt and 8 oz elbow macaroni. Cook for 8-10 minutes until al dente, stirring occasionally to prevent sticking. Test a piece at 8 minutes—it should be tender but firm, not mushy!
Drain the macaroni in a colander and set it aside, but do not rinse to keep the starch for a creamier sauce.
Heat 2 tbsp vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium heat until shimmering, about 1 minute. Add 1 tsp cumin seeds and let them sizzle for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add 1 medium finely chopped onion to the skillet and sauté for 4-5 minutes until soft and translucent, stirring frequently to avoid burning.
Stir in 2 minced garlic cloves, 1 tbsp grated ginger, and 1 finely chopped green chili (if using), cooking for 1 minute until aromatic. Keep the heat medium to prevent the garlic from turning bitter!
Sprinkle in 1 tsp turmeric powder, 1 tsp garam masala, and 1 tsp red chili powder, stirring constantly for 30 seconds to toast the spices and release their flavors.
Pour in 1 cup tomato puree and mix well, then simmer for 5 minutes over medium-low heat until the sauce thickens slightly and the raw tomato taste disappears.
Reduce the heat to low and stir in 1/2 cup heavy cream and 1/2 tsp salt, cooking for 2 minutes until the sauce is smooth and creamy. Avoid boiling after adding cream to prevent curdling!
Add the cooked macaroni to the skillet, tossing gently to coat every piece evenly with the masala sauce.
Sprinkle 1/2 cup shredded cheddar cheese over the top and cover the skillet for 2 minutes until the cheese melts slightly.
Remove from heat and garnish with 2 tbsp chopped cilantro before serving hot.
